content.problemStatement:
Chef and Chefina are at positions XXX and YYY on a number line.

They both love badminton.

It is known that badminton courts are located at every integer point.

They want to find a court such that the maximum distance travelled by either of them is **minimized**.

Formally, suppose they choose the badminton court at position ZZZ. You need to find the minimum value of maxŌüĪ(ŌłŻXŌłÆZŌłŻ,ŌłŻYŌłÆZŌłŻ)\max(|X-Z|, |Y-Z|)max(ŌłŻXŌłÆZŌłŻ,ŌłŻYŌłÆZŌłŻ) across all possible choices of ZZZ. Here, ŌłŻXŌłŻ|X|ŌłŻXŌłŻ denotes absolute value of XXX.

Report this minimum value.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two space-separated integers XXX and YYY.


Output Format
For each test case, output the minimum possible value of maxŌüĪ(ŌłŻXŌłÆZŌłŻ,ŌłŻYŌłÆZŌłŻ)\max(|X-Z|, |Y-Z|)max(ŌłŻXŌłÆZŌłŻ,ŌłŻYŌłÆZŌłŻ).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two spaceŌĆæseparated integers X and Y (X ŌēĀ Y), the positions of Chef and Chefina on the number line.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer ŌĆō the minimum possible value of max(|XŌĆæZ|, |YŌĆæZ|) over all integer court positions Z.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
For each pair (X,Y) we compute diff = |XŌĆæY| and answer = (diff+1)//2.
(3,5): diff=2 ŌåÆ (2+1)//2=1.
(7,6): diff=1 ŌåÆ (1+1)//2=1.
(1,10): diff=9 ŌåÆ (9+1)//2=5.
(63,31): diff=32 ŌåÆ (32+1)//2=16.

solution.approach: The value max(|XŌĆæZ|,|YŌĆæZ|) is minimized when Z is as close as possible to the midpoint of X and Y. For integer Z the optimal radius equals the ceiling of half the distance between X and Y. Hence answer = ceil(|XŌĆæY| / 2) = (|XŌĆæY| + 1) // 2. The algorithm for each test case: compute d = abs(XŌĆæY); output (d + 1) / 2 using integer division.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) ŌĆō constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) ŌĆō only a few variables.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        X = int(next(it))
        Y = int(next(it))
        diff = abs(X - Y)
        ans = (diff + 1) // 2
        out.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
